title: Did the kids really train for eva units at 7 years old
display_title: Did the kids really train for eva units at 7 years old
wikitext:
[]: Did the kids really train for eva units at 7 years old?             Quote Post by PurpleandRed » Sun Aug 23, 2015 8:33 am It says that the kids have been picked for training for eva units since around 2009, but why would they be training at only 7 years old? That would cause a lot of problems, kids who did stuff such as piloting that young were damaged. The kids would have to be a pre-teen to know about the dangers that could occur. So, unless they were training around 2011, then that would probably be more acceptable.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 23, 2015 9:24 am Asuka's been training since 2005, and Rei probably since she became Rei II. Shinji didn't start training until 2015, so I don't know where you're getting your info.

[]:             Quote Post by Glor » Sun Aug 23, 2015 9:29 am I don't think it's ever really mentioned whether or not they, Rei and Asuka, underwent very much training outside of sync testing. Even then two Evas weren't finished until 2014, with another completed early 2015. Asuka was selected as a pilot in 2005, but again, I don't think the "training" went much beyond "hey, sit in this big cylinder and let us half drown you in orange stuff." I assume they must've had simulation bodies for Asuka and Rei to practice with. Even then, they probably didn't have the means to do something like that until 2010, but I don't see the problem with starting them off younger if they could've.

[]:             Quote Post by PurpleandRed » Sun Aug 23, 2015 9:47 am Glor wrote: I don't think it's ever really mentioned whether of not they, Rei and Asuka, underwent very much training outside of sync testing. Even then two Evas weren't finished until 2014, with another completed early 2015. Asuka was selected as a pilot in 2005, but again, I don't think the "training" went much beyond "hey, sit in this big cylinder and let us half drown you in orange stuff." I assume they must've had simulation bodies for Asuka and Rei to practice with. Even then, they probably didn't have the means to do something like that until 2010, but I don't see the problem with starting them off younger if they could've.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 23, 2015 10:03 am PurpleandRed wrote: *Head-canon So, they had to be at least 9 when they started training, I consider a pre-teen 10 or 11

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Three » Sun Aug 23, 2015 10:43 am All the kids are damaged, as is just about everyone else. But isn't that one of the major themes of Evangelion anyway? The children are - for the most part - replaceable assets, so whether it's "acceptable" to put them in that position isn't really something NERV can care about. When it comes to making decisions, Ikari's living in a world where acceptability isn't exactly much of a factor. PurpleandRed wrote: *Head-canon

[]:             Quote Post by PurpleandRed » Sun Aug 23, 2015 11:03 am Blue Three wrote: All the kids are damaged, as is just about everyone else. But isn't that one of the major themes of Evangelion anyway? The children are - for the most part - replaceable assets, so whether it's "acceptable" to put them in that position isn't really something NERV can care about. When it comes to making decisions, Ikari's living in a world where acceptability isn't exactly much of a factor. Huh what? What is?

[]: Re: Did the kids really train for eva units at 7 years old?             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sun Aug 23, 2015 1:16 pm PurpleandRed wrote: That would cause a lot of problems, kids who did stuff such as piloting that young were damaged.

[]: Re: Did the kids really train for eva units at 7 years old?             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 23, 2015 1:24 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: If it's a choice between treating children nicely and global annihilation, world leaders are going to turn a very blind eye to any problems presented. It's not nice, but that's how it tends to be. And we can already see how the leadership of Seele was; there's no indication to show that they have any qualms about the damage this would cause anyone in the effort to achieve their goals.

[]:             Quote Post by PurpleandRed » Sun Aug 23, 2015 1:52 pm Yeah, and gotta hate those people on that show.

[]:             Quote Post by Seven Fifty » Mon Aug 24, 2015 6:50 am Training seven-year-olds to pilot the EVAs is pretty horrific, but then so is making fourteen-year-olds fight in them ... An organisation willing to do the second would no doubt also be willing to do the first.

[]: Not following. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Aug 24, 2015 7:02 am Glor wrote: Even then, they probably didn't have the means to do something like that until 2010,

[]:             Quote Post by Glor » Mon Aug 24, 2015 9:37 am ^It was a mock-up more than anything else. Still, 2010 is probably stretching it. I was assuming they'd want to focus resources getting the Evas finished first as opposed to wasting time on test-dummies. You're right though, they probably could've made them earlier than 2010.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Aug 24, 2015 9:49 am We know they did, because both Yui and Kyoko had Contact Experiments with their respective units by 2004 and 2005, respectively. That means a core for each had to exist, which in turn means there had to be something there for pilots to sync with. The gap between 2004 and 2015 is actually really puzzling; we know, for example, that Unit 02 was designed and built in Japan, but that final assembly (i.e. before the CE, i.e. in 2005) took place in Germany. So what the heck was being done with Unit 02 between then and now? Similarly, we know Unit 01 was split from Lilith in 2004, which means Unit 01 should have been good to go for all that time. And since Unit 00 was made before both, well . . . But anyway, this 2010 business came out of nowhere AFAICT. It might be relevant for Rei (since Rei II was created in 2010), but not for the other kids; as noted above Shinji didn't have anything to do with the project until 2015, and Asuka presumably performed an Activation Experiment with her Eva before being selected as a pilot (no point to selecting someone as a pilot if you don't even know if they can sync with the Eva), meaning she's been training (somehow) since 2005.

[]:             Quote Post by Glor » Mon Aug 24, 2015 11:39 am 2005-2015 is pretty ambiguous, but if it were as easy as just having the body ready before slapping armor on, it seems like they would've had a lot more Evas at the start of the show. Then somehow SEELE is able to make 9 Evas in the span of a few months. I don't actually remember how long it took them, but it was absurdly short by comparison.

[]:             Quote Post by Iuvenal » Mon Aug 24, 2015 11:46 am Bagheera wrote: Asuka presumably performed an Activation Experiment with her Eva before being selected as a pilot (no point to selecting someone as a pilot if you don't even know if they can sync with the Eva), meaning she's been training (somehow) since 2005.

[]:             Quote Post by PurpleandRed » Mon Aug 24, 2015 12:11 pm Bagheera wrote: We know they did, because both Yui and Kyoko had Contact Experiments with their respective units by 2005. That means a core for each had to exist, which in turn means there had to be something there for pilots to sync with. The gap between 2005 and 2015 is actually really puzzling; we know, for example, that Unit 02 was designed and built in Japan, but that final assembly (i.e. before the CE, i.e. in 2005) took place in Germany. So what the heck was being done with Unit 02 between then and now? Similarly, we know Unit 01 was split from Lilith in 2005, which means Unit 01 should have been good to go for all that time. And since Unit 00 was made before both, well . . . But anyway, this 2010 business came out of nowhere AFAICT. It might be relevant for Rei (since Rei II was created in 2010), but not for the other kids; as noted above Shinji didn't have anything to do with the project until 2015, and Asuka presumably performed an Activation Experiment with her Eva before being selected as a pilot (no point to selecting someone as a pilot if you don't even know if they can sync with the Eva), meaning she's been training (somehow) since 2005.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Aug 24, 2015 12:29 pm Iuvenal wrote: given the predominant theory that everyone in 2A has a dead mother whose soul is locked in a core somewhere

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Aug 24, 2015 12:31 pm Glor wrote: 2005-2015 is pretty ambiguous, but if it were as easy as just having the body ready before slapping armor on, it seems like they would've had a lot more Evas at the start of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Glor » Mon Aug 24, 2015 2:03 pm Bagheera wrote: Bear in mind that each Eva costs the GDP of a small country, and that the world was recovering from 2I and the loss of half its population while making the things. It took a while to get up to speed.

Article: Did the kids really train for eva units at 7 years old
 something there for pilots to sync with. The gap between 2004 and 2015 is actually really puzzling; we know, for example, that Unit 02 was designed and built in Japan, but that final assembly (i.e. before the CE, i.e. in 2005) took place in Germany. So what the heck was being done with Unit 02 between then and now? Similarly, we know Unit 01 was split from Lilith in 2004, which means Unit 01 should have been good to go for all that time. And since Unit 00 was made before both, well . . . But anyway, this 2010 business came out of nowhere AFAICT. It might be relevant for Rei (since Rei II was created in 2010), but not for the other kids; as noted above Shinji didn't have anything to do with the project until 2015, and Asuka presumably performed an Activation Experiment with her Eva before being selected as a pilot (no point to selecting someone as a pilot if you don't even know if they can sync with the Eva), meaning she's been training (somehow) since 2005.

[]:             Quote Post by Glor » Mon Aug 24, 2015 11:39 am 2005-2015 is pretty ambiguous, but if it were as easy as just having the body ready before slapping armor on, it seems like they would've had a lot more Evas at the start of the show. Then somehow SEELE is able to make 9 Evas in the span of a few months. I don't actually remember how long it took them, but it was absurdly short by comparison.
